sourceNote: TCGplayer Authentication Center study, read directly from the primary article (Peter Day, published 2025-01-17, updated 2025-01-27): more than 1,200 Prismatic Evolutions booster packs opened. Two nuances from the primary that republications miss. (1) Multi-SIR packs exist — 'Demigod Packs' with three SIRs and 'God Packs' with the full Eeveelution SIR line — so the SIR figure (~1 in 45) is the average COUNT of SIRs per pack, not the probability a pack contains one; TCGplayer states P(pack has >=1 SIR) is somewhat below 1/45 while the per-pack average holds. Our EV math uses expected count, so EV is computed correctly; the P(at least one SIR) shown per product is slightly OVERSTATED as a result. Their sample contained an estimated two Demigod packs and zero God packs (some SIR Eeveelutions were never pulled at all). (2) Structure, verified against the live catalog: no Illustration Rare tier exists (32 SIRs, 0 IRs, 180 cards); instead every C/UC/R has Poke Ball (~1 in 3) and Master Ball (~1 in 20) reverse variants, which are printing variants of existing cards and are unmodelled — EV slightly understates pack value. Version 1 rated this medium via republications; this is the primary, read directly.

alternateEstimates.note: A 200-pack community opening (the source concedes supply shortages prevented a significant sample) reports SIR ~1 in 53, ACE SPEC 1 in 29, Hyper Rare 1 in 152. Well within clustering error of the n=1,200 primary.
